name: Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail

description: Hike the moderate Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail, a 7.0-mile (11.3 km) route with 1019 feet (310 metres) of elevation gain, offering high mo
region_intro: Hiking around A Pobra De Trives offers routes through a landscape characterized by river valleys, forested hills, and open mountain areas. The region features varied terrain, including paths that lead to viewpoints and along reservoirs. Elevation changes are common, providing diverse challenges for hikers. Best hiking trails around A Pobra De Trives The most popular hiking route is Matacás Viewpoint Trail, a 10.0 miles (16.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 6 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views from the Matacás viewpoint. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.3 km) path. This trail winds through varied landscapes, including forested sections and open mountain areas. Local hikers also love the Route of the Glacial Valley of Forcadas – circular via the Chandrexa Reservoir, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through a glacial valley and alongside the Chandrexa Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 49 minutes. Hiking around A Pobra De Trives is defined by glacial valleys, river paths, and mountain viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in A Pobra De Trives are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore A Pobra De Trives's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around A Pobra De Trives?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes documented on komoot for the A Pobra De Trives region, offering a variety of experiences through river valleys, forested hills, and open mountain areas.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in A Pobra De Trives?
A: Yes, A Pobra De Trives offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935632" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Caldelas Castle loop from O Castro de Caldelas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.4 km (3.4 miles) trail that takes approximately 1 hour 36 minutes to complete, perfect for a relaxed walk.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in A Pobra De Trives?
A: Hike durations in A Pobra De Trives vary significantly. Shorter, easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Paisaxe Protexida Val do Río Navea&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under an hour, while more challenging trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984231586/matacas-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matacás Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; can take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in A Pobra De Trives?
A: Absolutely. Many of the easier routes are suitable for families. Look for trails with shorter distances and less elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38157875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Paisaxe Protexida Val do Río Navea&lt;/a&gt;, which is a gentle 2.7 km (1.7 miles) path.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around A Pobra De Trives?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in A Pobra De Trives. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations along your chosen route.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in A Pobra De Trives?
A: The best time for hiking in A Pobra De Trives is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is usually mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring cold temperatures and snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there circular walks available in A Pobra De Trives?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979329460/route-of-the-glacial-valley-of-forcadas-circular-via-the-chandrexa-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Glacial Valley of Forcadas – circular via the Chandrexa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a diverse landscape including a glacial valley and reservoir views.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in A Pobra De Trives?
A: Hiking trails in A Pobra De Trives offer diverse sights. You can explore natural landscapes like glacial valleys and river paths, as well as historical landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro Caldelas Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical attraction in the area.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the hikes?
A: Yes, the region is known for its expansive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984231586/matacas-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matacás Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for its panoramic vistas from the Matacás viewpoint, offering stunning perspectives of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in A Pobra De Trives?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to mountain areas, and the opportunities for both challenging ascents and more relaxed walks.

Q: Is parking generally available at trailheads in A Pobra De Trives?
A: While specific parking information varies by trailhead, many popular hiking routes in A Pobra De Trives have designated parking areas nearby. It's advisable to check the details for your chosen route on komoot, as users often share parking tips and locations.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore near the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, A Pobra De Trives is rich in history. You can find several interesting landmarks, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6560180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of Fátima and Fontei Cross&lt;/a&gt;, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6560182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Miguel de Montefurado&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible from or near hiking paths.
tips: The Manzaneda ski resort, also popularly called Cabeza de Manzaneda, is located in the eastern part of the province of Orense, a good place to disconnect in the middle of nature surrounded by all services.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided, but as the trail is located in the high mountain region of Galicia and culminates at a ski resort, it's likely that parking facilities are available near the Manzaneda Mountain Station, which serves as a common starting point for routes in the area.

faqs.question: Is the Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in high mountain areas where wildlife may be present or near the ski resort facilities. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a moderate hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The recommended hiking season for the Cabeza de Manzaneda area is typically between May and October. During these months, you can expect more favorable weather conditions for high mountain trekking, avoiding the snow and harsher conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Manzaneda Mountain Station?
faqs.answer: Hiking the Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail itself does not typically require specific permits or entrance fees. However, if you plan to use any facilities or services within the Manzaneda Mountain Station (such as the bike park or ski lifts), separate fees may apply.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a mix of terrain. While some sections consist of accessible paths, the terrain can become significantly tougher, particularly as you ascend closer to the ski area. The final section towards the communication tower at the summit is known for its scenic vistas and natural mountainous landscapes.

faqs.question: What are the main viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: A significant landmark on the route is the Cabeza de Manzaneda Summit. The presence of a communication tower at the peak suggests panoramic views from the highest points, offering impressive vistas of the surrounding countryside.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail?
faqs.answer: The Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail is approximately 11.3 km long with 310 meters of ascent and descent. It is estimated to take around 3 hours and 17 minutes to complete, assuming a moderate hiking pace.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other official paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the [CIMA OU06] Cabeza de Manzaneda * Ponte Bibei / Manzaneda (19%) and the [CIMA OU06] Cabeza de Manzaneda * Montefurado / Pobra de Trives (7%). A small portion also touches the Camiño da Mosenda (0%).

faqs.question: Is the Cabeza de Manzaneda Loop Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate and requires good fitness. While not overly technical, the length and elevation gain mean it's best suited for hikers with a reasonable fitness level and sure-footedness on some paths, rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The Cabeza de Manzaneda area is home to a ski resort, which typically means there are amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options available, especially within the Manzaneda Mountain Station complex. These can be found near the starting points of many trails in the region.

faqs.question: What should I consider bringing or wearing for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its high mountain setting, you should be prepared for varying weather. Bring appropriate layers, including waterproof and windproof outer shells, sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, and sun protection. A map or GPS device is also advisable for navigation.
